docs.title: Jeevan B. GC, PhD
docs.text: Senior AI Scientist — Peptide Design, AbbVie  USA  Contact · Google Scholar · GitHub · ResearchGate · LinkedIn · Curriculum Vitae

docs.title: About
docs.text:
I am a Senior AI Scientist working on peptide design at AbbVie. Before that, I was a Computational Chemistry Scientist at the Center for Protein Degradation, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, Boston, MA, where I used computational chemistry, biophysics, and single-particle cryo-EM data analysis to investigate small molecules targeting therapeutically important proteins.

My research combines physics-based molecular dynamics simulations, machine learning, statistical physics, and quantum mechanics to understand the motion and behavior of biomolecules — proteins, membranes, and small molecules.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: My research combines physics-based molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, machine learning, statistical physics, and quantum mechanics to understand the structure, dynamics, and function of biomolecules — proteins, membranes, and small molecules.

docs.title: Current Focus — Peptide Design (AbbVie)
docs.text: Computational and AI-driven approaches to peptide design, applying generative and physics-based modeling methods to therapeutic peptide discovery and optimization.

docs.title: Previous Focus — Protein Degradation & Drug Discovery (Dana-Farber Cancer Institute)
docs.text:
At the Center for Protein Degradation, my work centered on:

  • Computational chemistry & biophysics applied to small molecules targeting therapeutically important proteins
  • Single-particle cryo-EM data analysis integrated with computational modeling
  • Structure-based drug design workflows connecting simulation to experimental structural biology

docs.title: PhD Research — Computational Biophysics (Florida International University)
docs.text:
Working with Drs. Prem P. Chapagain and Bernard S. Gerstman, my doctoral research explored:

  • Transformer proteins and conformational transformation — using MD simulations to study how proteins like the transcription factor RfaH undergo large-scale structural rearrangements (α-helix to β-barrel transitions) that switch their function.
  • Ebola virus matrix protein VP40 — investigating how VP40 interacts with lipid membranes and phosphatidylserine, and how these interactions drive viral budding and assembly.
  • Membrane protein and GPCR ligand binding — studying how lipid bilayers influence ligand access to transmembrane binding sites in G protein-coupled receptors.

docs.title: Methods & Tools
docs.text:
• Molecular dynamics simulation (all-atom and coarse-grained, e.g., Martini force field)
  • Free energy / potential of mean force (PMF) calculations, including umbrella sampling
  • Machine learning and generative AI (e.g., denoising diffusion probabilistic models) for property prediction and molecular design
  • Cryo-EM data analysis
  • Structure-based and computer-aided drug design
